    • Regional simulation

Write AquaCrop Reference Evapotranspiration File

Source: R/write_eto.R
write_eto.Rd

Write an AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) reference evapotranspiration (.ETo) file containing daily ETo data for use in crop simulations.

Usage

write_eto(
  path = "CLIMATE/",
  site_name = NULL,
  data = NULL,
  var_name = "et0",
  syear = NULL,
  eyear = NULL,
  eol = NULL,
  record_type = 1,
  first_day = 1,
  first_month = 1
)

write_et0(
  path = "CLIMATE/",
  site_name = NULL,
  data = NULL,
  var_name = "et0",
  syear = NULL,
  eyear = NULL,
  eol = NULL,
  record_type = 1,
  first_day = 1,
  first_month = 1
)

Arguments

path

Directory path where the output .ETo file will be written. Default = "CLIMATE/"

site_name

Station name or identifier for the weather station. Default = NULL (extracted from data if available)

data

Data frame containing daily ETo data. Can have either:

  • Columns: station, year, month, day, et0 (legacy format), or

  • Columns: year, month, day, et0 (new format)

var_name

Name of the ETo column in the data frame. Default = "et0"

syear

Start year of the data period (extracted from data if not provided). Default = NULL

eyear

End year of the data period (extracted from data if not provided). Default = NULL

eol

End-of-line character style. Options: "windows","linux", or "macos". If NULL (default), eol is auto-detected. Options: "windows", "unix", "linux", or "macOS". Default = "windows"

record_type

Type of temporal aggregation. Options: 1 = daily, 2 = 10-daily, 3 = monthly. Default = 1

first_day

First day of record. For 10-daily: 1, 11, or 21; For monthly: 1. Default = 1

first_month

First month of record (1-12). Default = 1

Value

Invisibly returns the full path to the created .ETo file. Creates a formatted .ETo file in the specified directory with the format: <<site_name>>.ETo containing daily reference evapotranspiration data as required by AquaCrop v7.0.

See also

Other AquaCrop file writers: write_cal(), write_cal_batch(), write_cli(), write_cro(), write_gwt(), write_irr(), write_man(), write_obs(), write_off(), write_plu(), write_ppn(), write_prm(), write_swo(), write_tnx()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Load example weather data
data("weather")

# Write ETo file with default column name
write_eto(
  path = "weather/",
  site_name = "Wakanda_Station",
  data = weather
)

# Write ETo file with custom column name
write_eto(
  path = "weather/",
  site_name = "Wakanda_Station",
  data = weather,
  var_name = "eto_calculated"
)

# Legacy format (backward compatibility)
write_eto(data = weather_with_station, path = "climate/")
} # }
